title: Generate requirements for a battery-powered handheld vacuum
stakeholder_needs:
Generate requirements for a battery-powered handheld vacuum
    cleaner for home use. It should run for at least 20 minutes,
    weigh under 1.5 kg, and meet EU safety standards.
analysis: Stakeholders: end users (lightweight, runtime, suction, ease of emptying/cleaning, noise), purchasers (price/value, warranty), regulators/EU market access (CE marking, LVD/EMC/RoHS/REACH, battery transport), service/ops (filter/battery replacement, reliability), manufacturing/supply (cell availability, cost). Given fixed constraints (>=20 min runtime, <1.5 kg, EU safety), remaining key requirement gaps are performance definition (suction/airflow), operating modes & battery/charging expectations, and intended use cases/surfaces that drive attachments and filtration.
clarification_questions: What minimum cleaning performance should be specified (e.g., suction power in kPa, airflow in L/s, or pickup effectiveness on hard floor vs carpet)?
What charging and battery expectations apply (charging time, USB‑C vs dock, removable vs fixed battery, battery chemistry preference, and required cycle life)?
What are the primary home use cases/surfaces and debris types (e.g., kitchen crumbs, pet hair, sofa/car, liquids yes/no) and any target noise limit?

table.title: DFMEA – Battery-powered Handheld Vacuum Cleaner (Home Use, EU Safety)
rows: Provide safe energy storage; prevent thermal runaway; comply with UN38.3/IEC 62133
Cell internal short leading to thermal runaway
Fire/thermal event; user injury; property damage; product recall; non-compliance
Manufacturing defect; dendrite growth; mechanical crush/impact; overcharge due to BMS fault
Qualified cell supplier; cell screening; robust pack mechanical design; BMS with redundant OV/OT protections
Pack abuse tests; BMS HW/SW verification; incoming cell audits
Add secondary thermal fuse + improved cell spacing/venting; tighten cell incoming AQL; add crush guard ribs
Planned: add thermal cutoff in series; update supplier PPAP and audit plan
Design for venting path away from user; reuse BMS safety architecture across variants
Critical safety item; ensure CE technical file evidence
Provide correct SOC reporting and cutoff at UV/OV/OC/OT
BMS fails to cut off on over-discharge
Cell damage; reduced life; swelling; potential safety risk; runtime complaints
Firmware bug; ADC calibration drift; UV threshold mis-set; FET failure short
SW development process; calibration procedure; derating of MOSFETs; watchdog
EOL functional test; SW unit/integration tests; brownout test
Add independent UV comparator; add periodic ADC self-check; tighten EOL test limits and logging
Planned: add hardware UV supervisor and extend test coverage report
Independent hardware cutoff reduces reliance on firmware for safety/life
Focus on warranty returns due to early capacity loss
Charge safely under EU LVD/EMC; prevent overcharge and overheating
Battery overheating; reduced life; potential fire; non-compliance
Wrong CC/CV parameters; component tolerance stack; failed feedback optocoupler
Use certified adapter; parameter review; component derating; design FMEA on charger
Charge verification at temp extremes; safety tests; field monitoring
Add secondary OVP clamp; implement charge time-out and NTC plausibility check; increase supplier incoming test
Planned: add OVP crowbar and SW timeouts; update adapter spec
Prefer certified off-the-shelf adapters with full CB reports
Ensure CE docs include test reports and risk assessment
Provide sufficient airflow with efficiency to meet >=20 min runtime
Loss of suction; overheating; shortened runtime; user dissatisfaction
Hair/debris jam; bearing contamination; insufficient torque margin; driver current limit too low
Torque margin analysis; inlet screen; bearing seals; current limit tuning
Stall detection in firmware; thermal test; debris ingestion test
Increase torque margin; add anti-tangle inlet; improve stall recovery algorithm; update bearing sealing
Planned: add anti-tangle geometry and retune current control
Design for worst-case carpet/rug loads; include jam recovery requirement
Consider noise impact from higher torque settings
Maintain airflow and suction; avoid clogs during typical home debris pickup
Reduced suction; increased noise; motor overcurrent; runtime reduction
Sharp bends; undersized ducts; debris build-up; wet pickup misuse
CFD duct optimization; minimum cross-section rules; user manual warnings
Suction decay test with standardized debris; clogging trials
Increase duct radius; add clean-out access; add pre-separator; update nozzle geometry
Planned: prototype revised duct and separator; test with IEC dust mix
Provide easy-clean features to reduce returns and negative reviews
Ensure no new pinch points or sharp edges
Capture fine dust; maintain airflow; user-removable for cleaning
Dust emission; allergy issues; motor contamination; reduced performance; compliance risk
Poor pleat bonding; incorrect material; mishandling during cleaning; moisture exposure
Supplier qualification; material spec; edge potting; user instructions
Incoming inspection; burst/tear test; dust emission test
Add protective mesh; improve potting process; add washability limits and clearer markings
Planned: add mesh layer and update supplier process control plan
Design filters to survive user cleaning cycles; label replacement interval
Link to EU claims/marketing for filtration performance
Prevent air leaks to maintain suction and contain dust
Loss of suction; dust leakage; noise increase; customer complaints
Gasket compression set; tolerance stack; latch wear; contamination on seal
Tolerance analysis; gasket material selection; robust latch design
End-of-line suction test; leak check; life cycling
Add double-lip gasket; add poka-yoke seating feature; improve latch stiffness; add maintenance guidance
Planned: revise gasket profile and add seating boss; run 5k cycles test
Leak paths often due to assembly variability; add clear assembly work instructions
Monitor field returns for dust leakage patterns
Provide structural integrity; protect user from moving/energized parts; meet drop requirements
Exposed internals; sharp edges; loss of function; safety non-compliance
Insufficient ribbing; brittle resin; weld line weakness; screw boss stress
Drop analysis; resin selection; rib/boss design guidelines
Increase ribbing; add impact modifiers; redesign bosses; validate with multi-axis drop matrix
Planned: update CAD with ribs/boss fillets; order T1 samples for drop test
Design for repeated drops typical of handheld appliances
Ensure weight stays <1.5 kg after reinforcement
Allow user to operate reliably; provide accurate status indication
Cannot turn on/off; unintended run; battery drain; user frustration
Switch wear; contamination ingress; poor solder joints; misalignment
Component qualification; sealing; PCB support; assembly torque control
EOL button test; HALT; cycle test
Add higher-rated sealed switch; add debounce + fault detection; improve mechanical support
Planned: switch upgrade and PCB brace; add EOL cycle sampling
Switch selection critical for handheld duty cycles
Consider usability with gloves and wet hands
Provide truthful runtime/status information to manage >=20 min expectation
User perceives poor runtime; unexpected shutdown; returns
Poor SOC algorithm; cell aging not compensated; temperature effects
Algorithm calibration; temperature compensation; aging model
Runtime correlation test; field data review
Improve SOC algorithm with impedance tracking; add low-battery warning threshold tuning
Planned: collect discharge curves across temps and aging; update algorithm
Calibrate against real load profiles (carpet vs hard floor)
Align marketing claims with tested duty cycle
Provide reliable electrical connection; withstand cycles; prevent shock
Connector loosens or contact resistance increases
Intermittent charging; overheating at contacts; potential burn; warranty claims
Wear; contamination; plating wear; insufficient contact force; misalignment
Connector spec with cycle life; gold plating; alignment features
Thermal rise test; insertion/withdrawal cycle test; EOL charge verification
Increase contact force; add wipe action; add temperature monitoring near contacts; tighten alignment tolerances
Planned: new connector proposal and thermal sensor eval
Contact heating is common field failure; validate with worst-case adapters
Keep battery securely mounted; allow service replacement without hazards
Battery latch fails; pack ejects during use
Sudden shutdown; drop hazard; potential short if contacts exposed; injury risk
Weak latch; creep; poor engagement; tolerance stack; user impact
Latch design review; material selection; life-cycle testing
Retention pull test; drop with battery installed; EOL latch check
Add secondary latch feature; increase engagement depth; add wear indicator; improve contact shrouding
Planned: dual-action latch concept and prototype testing
Design for one-hand removal but robust retention
Meet EU EMC; survive ESD; avoid unintended operation
Unexpected stop/start; user dissatisfaction; potential safety concern if cannot stop
Insufficient ESD diodes; poor grounding; long traces to UI
ESD design rules; TVS selection; ground stitching; enclosure shielding strategy
IEC 61000-4-2 ESD test; pre-compliance EMC scans
Add TVS on key lines; improve ground return; add RC filtering on button lines; update layout
Planned: PCB layout changes and pre-scan booking
Do early pre-compliance to avoid late redesigns
Ensure CE marking supported by final test reports
Allow easy emptying; contain debris without spills
Dust spill; mess; user complaints; potential allergen exposure
Latch tolerance; vibration; wear; accidental press during use
Ergonomic review; latch guard; vibration testing
Vibration test; life cycling; user trials
Add latch guard; increase detent force; add secondary lock for transport; improve tactile feedback
Planned: guarded latch concept and user test A/B
User-centered latch design reduces returns and improves perceived quality

versions.analysis: Stakeholders: end users (lightweight, runtime, suction, ease of emptying/cleaning, noise), purchasers (price/value, warranty), regulators/EU market access (CE marking, LVD/EMC/RoHS/REACH, battery transport), service/ops (filter/battery replacement, reliability), manufacturing/supply (cell availability, cost). Given fixed constraints (>=20 min runtime, <1.5 kg, EU safety), remaining key requirement gaps are performance definition (suction/airflow), operating modes & battery/charging expectations, and intended use cases/surfaces that drive attachments and filtration.
